grammar.english: Each other / one another (mutual relationship between equals)
grammar.example: 朋友之间应该彼此信任。

grammar.english: So / like this / in such a way
grammar.example: 十年后，两座城市的发展状况如此不同。

grammar.pattern: Measure words: 册、朵、幅、届、颗、匹、扇、行、架、群、支、根、批、束、副、集、周
grammar.english: 册=volumes; 朵=flowers; 幅=paintings; 届=sessions/terms; 颗=small round objects; 匹=horses/cloth; 扇=doors/windows; 行=rows; 架=machines/planes; 群=groups; 支=sticks/pens; 根=long thin objects; 批=batches; 束=bundles; 副=pairs/sets; 集=episodes; 周=weeks
grammar.example: 一册书 / 一朵花 / 一幅画 / 一届学生 / 一颗糖 / 一匹马 / 一扇窗户

grammar.english: Verbal measure word: one glance/look
grammar.example: 他看了我一眼就走了。

grammar.english: Excessively / too (stronger and more formal than 太)
grammar.example: 这件事发生得过于突然了。

grammar.english: Really / so very (colloquial intensifier)
grammar.example: 他女朋友可漂亮了！

grammar.pattern: 稍 / 稍微 + Adj/Verb + 一点儿
grammar.english: Slightly / a little bit
grammar.example: 这幅画再挂得稍高一点儿。

grammar.english: Especially / particularly
grammar.example: 她喜欢运动，尤其是游泳。

grammar.english: Exceptionally / extremely (written/formal intensifiers)
grammar.example: 今天格外冷。/ 这项任务极其重要。

grammar.english: Mostly / for the most part (scope adverb)
grammar.example: 参加划船比赛的大都是女生。

grammar.english: From time to time / now and then
grammar.example: 我不时想起过去的事情。

grammar.pattern: 将 / 将要 / 即将 + Verb
grammar.english: Will / be about to (formal future markers)
grammar.example: 明年我们将去国外考察。/ 电视剧即将开始了。

grammar.english: Still / as before (unchanged state)
grammar.example: 二十年过去了，他仍旧没结婚。

grammar.pattern: 时常 / 老是 / 通常 + Verb
grammar.english: Often / frequently / usually (recurring action)
grammar.example: 她时常一个人出去散步。/ 他老是迟到。

grammar.english: Always / consistently (long-standing habit or trait)
grammar.example: 他一向对人很热情。

grammar.pattern: 立刻 / 连忙 / 急忙 + Verb
grammar.english: 立刻: immediately; 连忙: promptly react to something; 急忙: urgently due to worry or time pressure
grammar.example: 见到客人进来，他们连忙说'欢迎光临'。/ 还有五分钟就开会了，她急忙收拾好资料。

grammar.english: Gradually / little by little
grammar.example: 天渐渐黑了。

grammar.english: Both mean 'from beginning to end'; 始终 is more formal/literary; 一直 can refer to the future and combine with time words
grammar.example: 我们始终坚持正确的做法。/ 他不出来见我，我就一直在这里等。

grammar.english: As much as possible / as quickly as possible
grammar.example: 请尽量早点来。/ 请尽快回复。

grammar.english: It seems / as if (expressing a subjective impression)
grammar.example: 他似乎不太高兴。/ 她仿佛回到了童年。

grammar.pattern: 毕竟 / 居然 / 果然 / 简直
grammar.english: 毕竟: after all; 居然: surprisingly; 果然: sure enough; 简直: simply/utterly
grammar.example: 他毕竟是个孩子。/ 他居然通过了考试！/ 果然不出我所料。/ 这简直太好了！

grammar.english: On the contrary / instead (unexpected reversal)
grammar.example: 我帮了他，他反而生气了。

grammar.english: Ever since [time]
grammar.example: 自从来中国以来，他学了很多东西。

grammar.english: 朝: toward/facing (direction or object); 沿: along (a path or route)
grammar.example: 你朝前走，10分钟后就到了。/ 沿着这条路走就到了。

grammar.pattern: 朝 vs 向 vs 往
grammar.english: All mean 'toward'; 朝 & 向 can indicate facing; 往 focuses on destination of movement
grammar.example: 朝前走 / 向前看 / 往北走

grammar.pattern: 凭 / 据 / 依据 + Noun
grammar.english: On the basis of / according to / based on
grammar.example: 凭他的能力，一定能成功。/ 据报道，…

grammar.english: Written 'and': connects parallel items; earlier/more important item comes first; a pause before 以及 is allowed
grammar.example: 这篇论文介绍了背景、意义、现状以及影响。

grammar.pattern: 从而 / 因而 + Result clause
grammar.english: Thus / thereby / as a result (formal cause-effect connector)
grammar.example: 他努力学习，从而取得了好成绩。

grammar.english: It can be seen that / this shows that (drawing a logical conclusion)
grammar.example: 他连饭都顾不上吃，可见工作有多忙。

grammar.pattern: 假如 / 一旦 + Condition，（就）+ Result
grammar.english: 假如: if (hypothetical); 一旦: once / if ever (often for serious or irreversible events)
grammar.example: 假如明天下雨，比赛就取消。/ 一旦决定了，就不要后悔。

grammar.english: Both mean 'then/immediately after'; 便 is written only; 就 is both spoken and written
grammar.example: 如果你有问题，便/就来找我。

grammar.english: Either… or… (presenting alternatives)
grammar.example: 你或是今天来，或是明天来，都可以。

grammar.english: Nominalization: 'what is [verb]-ed' (formal written Chinese)
grammar.example: 别动，你所站的位置很危险！

grammar.english: '所' as a measure word for institutions/buildings
grammar.example: 这所大学已经有一百多年历史了。

grammar.english: Pivotal sentence: A causes/makes B feel or do something
grammar.example: 他的行为令我感到很失望。

grammar.english: Pivotal sentence: A (superior) dispatches B to do something
grammar.example: 经理派我去机场接客户。

grammar.pattern: A 不如/没有 B（这么/那么）+ Adj
grammar.english: Comparison: A is not as [adjective] as B
grammar.example: 深圳的历史不如/没有西安那么长。

grammar.english: Formal preposition with 5 uses: time (at), place (in), object (toward), starting point (from), comparison (than)
grammar.example: 中华人民共和国成立于1949年。/ 他致力于科学研究。

grammar.english: 突然 can modify nouns and take degree adverbs (很突然); 忽然 is adverbial only
grammar.example: 这是一起突然的事故。/ 我忽然明白了。

grammar.english: Both mean 'fortunately'; 多亏 = grateful (thanks to a person); 幸亏 = lucky relief (due to an objective condition)
grammar.example: 多亏了你，我才完成了任务。/ 幸亏带了雨伞，没淋湿。

grammar.english: In a hurry / hastily (adjective, takes degree adverbs)
grammar.example: 昨天走得太匆忙，没来得及和你说一声。

grammar.pattern: 不但不/没有 A，反而 B
grammar.english: Not only did A not happen, but the opposite (B) occurred instead
grammar.example: 吃了这药，感冒不但没好，反而更严重了。

grammar.english: Would rather A than B (choosing the lesser of two undesirable options)
grammar.example: 我宁可离婚，也不愿意将就过日子。

grammar.english: Would rather endure A in order to do/have B
grammar.example: 小孩子宁可不吃饭，也要继续玩。

grammar.english: Rather than A, it's better to B (speaker prefers B)
grammar.example: 与其坐火车，不如坐飞机更快。

grammar.english: In the unlikely/unfortunate event that A, then B (low-probability contingency)
grammar.example: 万一他不在家，你就自己开门。

grammar.english: Fortunately A happened; otherwise B (bad outcome) would have occurred
grammar.example: 幸亏车来了，不然我们都得迟到。

grammar.english: Unless A (the only condition), otherwise B will happen
grammar.example: 除非是他邀请我，不然我是不会去的。

grammar.english: Even if A (hypothetical extreme), B remains true or unchanged
grammar.example: 哪怕只有一个观众，她也要演下去。

grammar.english: Formal passive voice: [subject] is [verb]-ed by [noun]
grammar.example: 法官为这个孩子的诚实所感动。

grammar.english: Omitting the subject when it is already clear from context
grammar.example: （我）决定出国留学，（我）不得不和父母告别。

grammar.english: Omitting the object when it is recoverable from context
grammar.example: 语言的变化，短时间内不容易发现（这些变化），日子长了就表现出来了。

topics.label: Daily Life
topics.description: Daily affairs, dining, transportation, shopping, medical & mental health, leisure, community, and family life

topics.label: Education
topics.description: Academic courses, teaching and learning, campus life, and family, school, social and vocational education

topics.label: Workplace & Career
topics.description: Work content, professional experience, teamwork, personnel management, and enterprise development

topics.label: Nature & Environment
topics.description: Natural resources, terrain, natural phenomena, flora and fauna, ecological balance, urban/rural environments, and environmental protection

topics.label: Science & Technology
topics.description: Product introductions and applications, popular science knowledge, and scientific research achievements

topics.label: Contemporary Society
topics.description: Demographics, characteristics of the era, trade and commerce, economic development, social trends, infrastructure, arts and culture, sports, and China-foreign exchanges

topics.label: Culture & Tradition
topics.description: Language and script, folk customs, traditional festivals, cultural landscapes, philosophical thought, traditional arts, history, and ancient science and technology
